proposed_hypotheses.text: In the ds001_prostate cohort, the overall objective_response rate is non-zero and varies meaningfully across patient subgroups (i.e., this dataset has signal, not a constant outcome).

analyses.result_summary: Overall objective_response rate = 0.2402 (n=50000). ECOG distribution 0/1/2 = 17592/24971/7437. mCRPC 55.0%, visceral mets 19.9%, PSMA-high 59.9%, BRCA2 10.0%, AR-V7+ 20.1%, MSI-high 3.1%. Outcome and biomarker distributions are non-degenerate.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with brca2_mutation=1 have a LOWER objective_response rate than brca2_mutation=0.
Patients with ar_v7_positive=1 have a LOWER objective_response rate than ar_v7_positive=0.
Patients with msi_high=1 have a LOWER objective_response rate than msi_high=0.
Patients with psma_high=1 have a HIGHER objective_response rate than psma_high=0.

analyses.result_summary: BRCA2+: 15.0% (n=4996) vs BRCA2-: 25.0% (n=45004). Chi-square p=4.1e-56. Direction: lower response with BRCA2.
AR-V7+: 16.0% (n=10038) vs AR-V7-: 26.0% (n=39962). Chi-square p=1.4e-98. Direction: lower response with AR-V7+.
MSI-high: 17.6% (n=1528) vs MSI-stable: 24.2% (n=48472). Chi-square p=2.5e-9. Direction: lower response with MSI-high overall.
PSMA-high: 23.9% vs PSMA-low: 24.3%. Chi-square p=0.30. Direction opposite to predicted; NS.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher ecog_ps is associated with LOWER objective_response.
mcrpc=1 patients have LOWER objective_response than mcrpc=0.
visceral_mets=1 patients have LOWER objective_response than visceral_mets=0.
Higher gleason_score is associated with LOWER objective_response.
Older age_years is associated with LOWER objective_response.
Higher psa_ng_ml is associated with LOWER objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Per ECOG unit, logit coef = -0.285 (OR=0.752), p=9.5e-73. Direction confirmed: higher ECOG -> lower response.
mCRPC=1: 15.4% vs mCRPC=0: 34.6% (RD=-19.2%, chi-square p~0). Strongest single risk factor for non-response.
Visceral+: 24.0% vs visceral-: 24.0%, p=0.86. NS univariately.
Coef=+0.0053, p=0.61. NS; no association.
Mean age 65.0 (responders) vs 65.0 (non-responders), p=0.82. NS.
Mean PSA 28.2 (responders) vs 47.2 (non-responders), p=1.5e-137. Direction confirmed: higher PSA in non-responders.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher albumin_g_dl predicts HIGHER objective_response (better nutritional status).
Higher ldh_u_l predicts LOWER objective_response.
Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo predicts LOWER objective_response.
Higher crp_mg_l predicts LOWER objective_response.
Higher nlr (neutrophil-lymphocyte ratio) predicts LOWER objective_response.
Higher hemoglobin_g_dl predicts HIGHER objective_response.
Higher alkaline_phosphatase_u_l predicts LOWER objective_response (bone burden).

analyses.result_summary: Mean albumin 3.82 (resp) vs 3.80 (non), diff=+0.024 g/dL, p=3.6e-6. Direction confirmed.
Mean LDH 223.8 vs 224.1, p=0.68. NS.
Mean weight loss 3.50% (resp) vs 3.95% (non), diff=-0.45%, p=6.8e-31. Direction confirmed: more weight loss -> non-response.
Mean CRP 5.69 (resp) vs 6.23 (non), diff=-0.53, p=9.3e-9. Direction confirmed.
Mean NLR 3.49 vs 3.50, p=0.61. NS.
Mean hemoglobin 12.50 vs 12.51, p=0.51. NS.
Mean alk phos 104.7 vs 104.2, p=0.34. NS.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_enzalutamide=1 is associated with HIGHER objective_response than treatment_enzalutamide=0.
treatment_abiraterone=1 is associated with HIGHER objective_response.
treatment_docetaxel=1 is associated with HIGHER objective_response.
treatment_olaparib=1 is associated with HIGHER objective_response.
treatment_lu177_psma=1 is associated with HIGHER objective_response.
treatment_pembrolizumab=1 is associated with HIGHER objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Enza: 36.1% (n=20076) vs 15.9% (n=29924), RD=+20.2%, p~0. Massive main effect, direction confirmed.
Abi: 23.8% vs 24.1%, p=0.46. No main effect.
Doce: 24.0% vs 24.0%, p=0.91. No main effect.
Olap: 23.9% vs 24.0%, p=0.87. No main effect overall (suggests biomarker-restricted use needed).
Lu177: 24.3% vs 24.0%, p=0.59. No main effect.
Pembro: 23.9% vs 24.0%, p=0.86. No main effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_olaparib produces a LARGER absolute increase in objective_response in brca2_mutation=1 than in brca2_mutation=0 (PARP-inhibitor synthetic lethality hypothesis).

analyses.code: stratified RD: olaparib by brca2; logit interaction
analyses.result_summary: BRCA2-: olap 25.3% vs 25.0% (RD=+0.3%). BRCA2+: olap 12.1% (n=529) vs 15.3% (n=4467) (RD=-3.2%). Interaction coef=-0.289, p=0.045. Direction OPPOSITE to predicted: olaparib appears slightly HARMFUL in BRCA2-mutant patients.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_pembrolizumab produces a LARGER absolute increase in objective_response in msi_high=1 than in msi_high=0.

analyses.code: stratified RD: pembro by msi_high; logit interaction
analyses.result_summary: MSI-stable: pembro 24.1% vs 24.2% (RD=-0.0015). MSI-high: pembro 17.7% (n=79) vs 17.6% (n=1449) (RD=+0.0012). Interaction coef=+0.017, p=0.96. NS; pembro shows no incremental benefit in MSI-high.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_lu177_psma produces a LARGER absolute increase in objective_response in psma_high=1 than in psma_high=0.

analyses.code: stratified RD: lu177 by psma_high; logit interaction
analyses.result_summary: PSMA-low: lu177 25.0% vs 24.1% (RD=+0.0089). PSMA-high: lu177 23.8% vs 23.9% (RD=-0.0011). Interaction p=0.36. NS.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The benefit of treatment_enzalutamide on objective_response is SMALLER in ar_v7_positive=1 than in ar_v7_positive=0 (AR-V7 splice variant confers AR-targeted therapy resistance).
The (lack of) effect of treatment_abiraterone differs by ar_v7_positive status.

analyses.code: stratified RD: enzalutamide by ar_v7_positive; logit interaction
analyses.result_summary: AR-V7-: enza 41.1% vs 16.0% (RD=+25.1%). AR-V7+: enza 16.5% vs 15.7% (RD=+0.85%). Interaction coef=-1.237, p=1.4e-93. Direction confirmed strongly: AR-V7 abolishes the enzalutamide benefit.

analyses.code: stratified RD: abiraterone by ar_v7_positive; logit interaction
analyses.result_summary: AR-V7-: abi 25.7% vs 26.2% (RD=-0.005). AR-V7+: abi 16.3% vs 15.8% (RD=+0.005). Interaction p=0.33. No abi effect in either stratum.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The effect of treatment_enzalutamide on objective_response is SMALLER at higher ecog_ps.
The (null) effects of abi/doce/olaparib/lu177/pembro do not vary with ecog_ps.

analyses.code: Logit objective_response ~ enza * ecog_ps + by-ECOG RDs
analyses.result_summary: RD by ECOG: 0=+20.7%, 1=+20.1%, 2=+19.8%. Interaction coef=+0.141, p=1.6e-5 (statistically significant but absolute effect almost flat across ECOG; ECOG itself is a strong main predictor).

analyses.code: Logit interactions: each non-enza treatment * ecog_ps
analyses.result_summary: abi: p=0.91; doce: p=0.76; olaparib: p=0.99; lu177: p=0.82; pembro: p=0.07. None significant; no ECOG-driven heterogeneity for these treatments.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The benefit of treatment_enzalutamide is LARGER in mcrpc=0 than in mcrpc=1 (the AR axis is still hormone-naive in mcrpc=0).
The other treatments show no mCRPC-by-treatment interaction.

analyses.code: stratified RD: enza by mcrpc; logit interaction
analyses.result_summary: mCRPC=0: enza 60.96% (n=9039) vs 16.93% (n=13480), RD=+44.0%. mCRPC=1: enza 15.81% vs 15.05%, RD=+0.77%. Interaction coef=-1.978, p~0. mCRPC abolishes enza benefit; effect concentrated in hormone-sensitive disease.

analyses.code: Logit interactions: each non-enza treatment * mcrpc
analyses.result_summary: abi p=0.31, doce p=0.63, olap p=0.62, lu177 p=0.57, pembro p=0.37. No significant interactions.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The benefit of treatment_enzalutamide is LARGER in visceral_mets=0 than visceral_mets=1.
The benefit of treatment_lu177_psma is SMALLER in visceral_mets=1 than visceral_mets=0.

analyses.code: stratified RD: enza by visceral; logit interaction
analyses.result_summary: Visceral=0: enza RD=+20.30%. Visceral=1: enza RD=+20.01%. Interaction p=0.86. NS — visceral mets is NOT a meaningful modifier of enza effect.

analyses.code: stratified RD: lu177 by visceral; logit interaction
analyses.result_summary: Visceral=0: lu177 24.9% vs 23.9% (RD=+0.99%). Visceral=1: lu177 21.9% vs 24.3% (RD=-2.43%). Interaction coef=-0.191, p=0.010. Modest negative interaction (lu177 trends worse with visceral mets).

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a multivariable logistic model controlling for all features and treatments, treatment_enzalutamide remains the only treatment with a significant, positive coefficient.

In the same multivariable model, mcrpc, ecog_ps, brca2_mutation, ar_v7_positive, msi_high, weight_loss_pct_6mo, crp_mg_l, and psa_ng_ml retain independent NEGATIVE associations with objective_response.

analyses.code: Logit objective_response ~ all features + all treatments
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted enza coef=+1.201 (OR=3.32), p~0. Other treatment coefs: abi -0.025 (p=0.31), doce +0.002 (p=0.93), olaparib +0.025 (p=0.50), lu177 +0.029 (p=0.35), pembro +0.014 (p=0.79). Only enzalutamide significant.

analyses.code: Logit objective_response ~ all features + all treatments — non-treatment coefficients
analyses.result_summary: mCRPC -1.125 (p~0), AR-V7 -0.695 (p=7.3e-109), BRCA2 -0.735 (p=5.6e-64), MSI-high -0.431 (p=2.6e-9), ECOG -0.327 (p=1.6e-82), weight loss -0.036/% (p=1.6e-33), CRP -0.0073/mg-L (p=8.6e-8), PSA -0.001 (p=2.9e-8), albumin +0.127 (p=1.7e-8). All directions match priors except albumin is positive (good).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Across a screen of treatment x feature logistic interactions, the strongest treatment effect-modifiers will be: (a) enzalutamide x mcrpc (negative), (b) enzalutamide x ar_v7_positive (negative), (c) enzalutamide x brca2_mutation (negative), (d) enzalutamide x msi_high (negative).

analyses.code: 6 treatments * 18 modifiers; rank by interaction p
analyses.result_summary: Top interactions: enza x mcrpc (coef=-1.978, p~0), enza x ar_v7_positive (coef=-1.237, p=1.4e-93), enza x psa_ng_ml (coef=-0.0087/PSA-unit, p=1.2e-72), enza x brca2_mutation (coef=-1.138, p=3.3e-42), enza x msi_high (coef=-1.283, p=1.8e-19), enza x ecog (small +, p=1.6e-5), lu177 x visceral (coef=-0.19, p=0.010), olaparib x brca2 (coef=-0.289, p=0.045). All four predicted enza interactions are confirmed; an unexpected olaparib x BRCA2 negative interaction is also flagged.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within brca2_mutation=1 patients, treatment_olaparib remains associated with LOWER objective_response across ECOG strata.
Within brca2_mutation=1 patients, treatment_olaparib remains associated with LOWER objective_response in BOTH visceral_mets=0 and visceral_mets=1.

analyses.code: olaparib RD within BRCA2+ stratified by ECOG
analyses.result_summary: BRCA2+ ECOG=0: olap 15.3% (n=183) vs 19.7% (n=1551), RD=-4.4%. ECOG=1: 8.9% vs 13.8%, RD=-4.9%. ECOG=2: 14.9% vs 10.2%, RD=+4.7% (small n=87 on). Effect is negative in ECOG 0/1 strata.

analyses.code: olaparib RD within BRCA2+ stratified by visceral_mets
analyses.result_summary: BRCA2+ visceral=0: olap 12.0% vs 15.2%, RD=-3.2%. Visceral=1: 12.4% vs 15.7%, RD=-3.3%. Direction consistent across visceral status.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within msi_high=1, treatment_pembrolizumab effect is HIGHER in mcrpc=0 than in the overall MSI-high group (i.e., pembro benefit, if any, concentrates in hormone-sensitive MSI-high disease).

analyses.code: pembro RD within MSI-high * (mCRPC, ECOG, visceral)
analyses.result_summary: MSI-high & mCRPC=0: pembro 29.4% (n=34) vs 19.4% (n=655), RD=+10.0% (small n on, suggestive but underpowered). MSI-high & mCRPC=1: trivial. MSI-high * ECOG: ECOG=0 RD=-1.7%, ECOG=1 RD=+2.6%, ECOG=2 RD=-5.3%. Inconsistent and underpowered; no robust subgroup of MSI-high pembro benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within psma_high=1, treatment_lu177_psma produces no meaningful response benefit in any (mCRPC, ECOG, visceral) subgroup.

analyses.code: lu177 RD within PSMA-high stratified by mCRPC, ECOG, visceral
analyses.result_summary: PSMA-high RDs: ECOG=0 -0.5%, ECOG=1 +0.8%, ECOG=2 -2.2%; visceral=0 +0.4%, visceral=1 -2.0%; mcrpc=0 -1.2%, mcrpc=1 +0.9%. All small (|RD|<0.025) and inconsistent. No robust beneficial subgroup found.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within ar_v7_positive=0, treatment_enzalutamide produces a LARGE positive RD (>20 percentage points) regardless of ecog_ps.

analyses.code: enza RD within AR-V7- by ECOG
analyses.result_summary: AR-V7- & ECOG=0: enza 45.9% vs 20.0% (RD=+25.9%, n_on=5610). ECOG=1: 39.4% vs 14.8% (RD=+24.6%). ECOG=2: 35.2% vs 10.3% (RD=+24.9%). Enzalutamide benefit in AR-V7- patients is large and stable across ECOG strata.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is NO subgroup of brca2_mutation=1 patients (defined by ECOG and visceral_mets) in which treatment_olaparib produces a positive RD on objective_response of clinically meaningful magnitude.

analyses.code: olaparib RD within BRCA2+ stratified by ECOG and visceral
analyses.result_summary: BRCA2+ & ECOG<=1 & visceral=0: olap 11.0% (n=365) vs 16.0% (n=3033), RD=-5.1%. BRCA2+ & ECOG>=2: olap 14.9% vs 10.2%, RD=+4.7% (small n). Six full ECOGxvisceral cells: 5/6 are negative or near-zero RDs. Hypothesis supported: no robust positive olaparib benefit subgroup within BRCA2+ patients.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The entire treatment_enzalutamide benefit is concentrated in mcrpc=0 patients (no benefit in mcrpc=1) and is INDEPENDENT of visceral_mets status.

analyses.code: enza x mcrpc and enza x visceral interactions revisited
analyses.result_summary: Enza x mCRPC: mcrpc=0 RD=+44.0%, mcrpc=1 RD=+0.77% (interaction p~0). Enza x visceral: visceral=0 RD=+20.30%, visceral=1 RD=+20.01% (interaction p=0.86). Confirmed: mCRPC drives the heterogeneity; visceral does not.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Neither treatment_docetaxel nor treatment_abiraterone shows clinically meaningful effect heterogeneity on objective_response across mcrpc, visceral_mets, or ecog_ps.

analyses.code: interactions for doce and abi vs mcrpc/visceral/ecog
analyses.result_summary: All six interactions p>0.30 with stratified RDs |<0.01|. abi and doce are essentially inert on objective_response in this dataset across these splits. Hypothesis supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Even in the most permissive subgroup (psma_high=1, ECOG=0, visceral_mets=0), treatment_lu177_psma does not produce a clinically meaningful positive RD on objective_response.

analyses.code: lu177 RD within PSMA-high * ECOG * visceral
analyses.result_summary: PSMA-high & ECOG=0 & visc=0: lu177 27.9% (n=1266) vs 27.9% (n=7360), RD=-0.0001. All 6 cells |RD|<=0.027. No meaningful benefit subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The most plausible pembrolizumab benefit subgroup is msi_high=1 & ecog_ps<=1 & visceral_mets=0 (immunotherapy works best in fit, MSI-high, non-visceral patients), but the dataset is too underpowered to confirm.

analyses.code: pembro RD in MSI-high * ECOG * visceral cells
analyses.result_summary: MSI-high cells with pembro on/off: ECOG=0 visc=0: 23.8% vs 20.7% (RD=+3.1%, n_on=21). ECOG=1 visc=0: 16.7% vs 14.8% (RD=+1.8%, n_on=36). ECOG=1 visc=1: 20.0% vs 12.7% (RD=+7.3%, n_on=5). Generally small positive RDs in non-ECOG2 cells but tiny n_on; cannot conclude.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is NO 2-feature ECOG x visceral_mets cell within brca2_mutation=1 in which treatment_olaparib produces a positive RD with adequate sample size; the apparent absence of olaparib benefit in BRCA2+ patients is robust.

analyses.code: olaparib RD in BRCA2+ * ECOG * visceral cells
analyses.result_summary: BRCA2+ ECOG=0 visc=0 RD=-4.9%, ECOG=0 visc=1 RD=-1.7%, ECOG=1 visc=0 RD=-5.3%, ECOG=1 visc=1 RD=-3.2%, ECOG=2 visc=0 RD=+7.3% (n_on=67), ECOG=2 visc=1 RD=-3.7% (n_on=20). 5/6 cells negative. Hypothesis supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A shallow decision tree fit on the treated subset of each treatment will identify, for treatment_enzalutamide, a subgroup defined by mcrpc=0 AND ar_v7_positive=0 AND brca2_mutation=0 with response rate ~78% on enzalutamide vs ~17% off (RD ~+60 percentage points). For the other five treatments, no analogous high-RD subgroup will exist.

FINAL best-supported treatment-effect subgroup for treatment_enzalutamide and objective_response: in patients with mcrpc=0 AND ar_v7_positive=0, treatment_enzalutamide HIGHER objective_response than no enzalutamide. Suppressors: mcrpc=1 and ar_v7_positive=1 each independently abolish the benefit.

FINAL best-supported subgroup for treatment_olaparib and objective_response: there is NO subgroup with a positive RD; the closest 'expected' subgroup brca2_mutation=1 actually shows a small NEGATIVE RD.

FINAL best-supported subgroup for treatment_pembrolizumab and objective_response: among msi_high=1 & mcrpc=0 patients, pembrolizumab is associated with HIGHER objective_response (29% vs 19%, n_on=34); evidence is suggestive but not statistically conclusive.

FINAL best-supported subgroup for treatment_lu177_psma and objective_response: NO subgroup with a clinically meaningful positive RD; the expected psma_high=1 subgroup is null. Modest negative interaction with visceral_mets.

FINAL best-supported subgroups for treatment_abiraterone and treatment_docetaxel and objective_response: NO subgroup with detectable positive RD; both treatments are essentially inert on this outcome in this cohort.

analyses.code: DecisionTreeClassifier(max_depth=3) on treated subset for each treatment; then within-leaf RD vs untreated controls
analyses.result_summary: Enza tree leaf 3 (mcrpc<=0.5 & ar_v7<=0.5 & brca2<=0.5): on=78.0% (n=6514), off=17.3% (n=9661), RD=+60.7%. Other treatments: best-leaf RDs all <=+0.06 (abi 0.034, doce 0.023, olap 0.050, lu177 0.037, pembro 0.059); none approaches the enza signal.

mcrpc=0 & AR-V7-: enza 71.7% (n=7246) vs 17.1% (n=10754), RD=+54.6%. Adding ECOG<=1: 72.9% vs 18.0%, RD=+54.8% (no further refinement needed). Suppressors confirmed: mcrpc=1 cell RD=+0.77%; AR-V7+ cell RD=+0.85%.
BRCA2+: olap RD=-3.2% (p=0.045 on interaction). All 2- and 3-feature subgroups within BRCA2+ confirm absent or negative olaparib effect. No positive subgroup.
MSI-high & mCRPC=0: pembro 29.4% (n=34) vs 19.4% (n=655), RD=+10.0%. Suggestive only; n_on=34 is too small for definitive conclusion.
All PSMA-high cells have RD between -0.027 and +0.014; main interaction with visceral mets significant but small (lu177 worse with visceral).

analyses.code: Abi/doce stratified analyses across mCRPC, visceral, ECOG, biomarkers
analyses.result_summary: Abi main effect RD=-0.003 (p=0.46); doce main RD=-0.0005 (p=0.91). All examined interactions p>0.30 with |RD|<0.01. Both treatments inert.
